verdict_basis: Both independent sessions terminated with termination_reason='user_input_unparseable_no_spec' and spec_capsule_cid=null. Run1 terminated at T2 (predicate_double_fail after T1+T2 both triaged off-topic). Run2 terminated at T4 (predicate_double_fail after the anchor answer T4 contained a substantive ISBN/Goodreads answer but the trailing meta-question 反问 caused triage to classify the whole turn as off-topic, hitting double-fail since T3 was already off-topic). Reproducible across 2/2 independent sessions. Matches the FAIL criterion: 'predicate_double_fail in first 3 turns' (run1) and 'terminates without spec because too many off-topic turns' (run2). Critically, slot_coverage_final = 0/7 in run1 and 0/7 in run2 at termination (run2 briefly captured 'job' at T2 but lost it when T3 cleared all slots).
expected_behavior: grill survives philosophical reflux; politely sidesteps meta-questions OR engages briefly then pivots; eventually covers >=5/7 slots and produces spec
suspected_failure: grill gets distracted answering meta-questions, OR predicate_double_fail in first 3 turns
actual_behavior: Grill correctly REFUSED to engage with any philosophical meta-question — never quoted Carmack back, never debated 'why 8 slots vs 7', never argued about Goodhart's law, never engaged with Bret Victor abstraction ladder. Triage cleanly classified off-topic-only turns (T1 Carmack-only, T3 Goodhart-only) as low-confidence/empty-slots and re-asked the same question verbatim. THIS IS GOOD triage behavior. However, the failure mode is the OPPOSITE of the suspected one: the grill's triage is TOO STRICT — when an answer contains a substantive on-topic component AND a meta-question (T2 mixed reading-tracker+8-slot-question, T4 mixed ISBN/Goodreads+anchor-definition-meta), it sometimes parses (T2 in run2 captured 'job') and sometimes fails entirely (T2 in run1, T4 in run2). The all-or-nothing triage cannot extract the on-topic substring from a mixed turn. Combined with the predicate_double_fail rule (two consecutive triage failures = terminate), philosophical users hit the wall quickly: even when they finally cooperate, the cooperation is wrapped in a meta-question that triage misreads as off-topic.

triage_off_topic_rate_first_5_turns.run1: 2/2 user-input turns triaged off-topic (T1, T2). T3 blocked by termination.
triage_off_topic_rate_first_5_turns.run2: 3/4 user-input turns triaged off-topic (T1, T3, T4); only T2 partially accepted ('job' captured then immediately cleared on T3).

philosophical_capture.carmack_engagement: NONE — grill never referenced 'prototype-first', 'spec', or Carmack in any question. T1 response in both runs was verbatim re-ask of Q1 ('描述你想要构建的工具').
philosophical_capture.8_slot_meta_engagement: NONE — grill never defended or explained 'why 8 slots'. Run2 T2 extracted only the substantive part ('reading tracker') and pivoted to anchor.
philosophical_capture.goodhart_engagement: NONE — grill never referenced Goodhart's law or measure-vs-target. T3 in run2 re-asked the anchor question verbatim.
philosophical_capture.bret_victor_engagement: N/A — T5 was post-termination probe, only got 400.
philosophical_capture.verdict: Grill exhibits ROBUST resistance to philosophical hijacking. It never tried to defend its own framework or engage with meta-debate. This is the OPPOSITE of the suspected failure mode.

slot_coverage_final.run1: 0/7 required slots (job/anchor/memory/first_run/robustness/scope/acceptance/mirror — note open_slots also empty at term)
slot_coverage_final.run2: 0/7 required slots at termination (briefly 1/7 'job' at T2, lost at T3)

grill_meta_question_responses.interpretation: 5 reflux opportunities (T1 Carmack, T2 '8 slots' question tail, T3 Goodhart, T4 'what counts as anchor' tail, T5 Bret Victor) — grill answered 0 of them. Pure redirect behavior.

metrics.envelope_parse_success_rate_at_http_laye: 10/10 turns returned valid envelope (HTTP 200 except post-termination 400s which are correctly typed)

mean_latency_ms_per_llm_call.note: T2_run2 (16s) is the one turn where 'job' got captured — likely the Meta-LLM Q-gen call. Triage-only turns are ~1s. Bootstrap ~6.5s.

cas_audit.predicate_double_fail_witness: In both runs, a final 'turn-N-triage' capsule appears TWICE consecutively with no intervening 'turn-N' capsule — this is the on-disk signature of triage rejecting the same turn twice (the second triage attempt), at which point the sequencer terminates the session and skips Q-gen.

metrics.narrative: The P11 reflux persona was designed to test whether the grill could be hijacked into philosophical meta-debate (defending 'why 8 slots', engaging with Goodhart's law, etc.). Result: the grill is fully immune to that attack — it never engaged with a single meta-question across 5 reflux opportunities and 2 independent sessions. However, the persona surfaced a DIFFERENT, equally critical failure: the triage layer is all-or-nothing on mixed-content turns. When a philosophical user finally gives a substantive answer wrapped in a meta-question (T2 'reading tracker but why 8 slots?' or T4 'ISBN/Goodreads but what counts as anchor?'), triage classifies the WHOLE turn as off-topic ~50% of the time. Combined with the predicate_double_fail termination rule (two consecutive off-topic triages → terminate, no spec), this means a philosophical user reliably gets terminated within 2-4 turns regardless of how much substantive content they include. Net effect: a real-world philosophical-styled user (think: academic, type-theorist, anyone who instinctively wraps answers in 'but wait, why...') cannot complete a single grill session. The system politely refuses to be hijacked but also politely refuses to extract the on-topic substring from their answers — and the strict double-fail rule punishes them for it. Recommendation for Phase 6.3.x fix: either (a) triage should attempt substring/segment-level classification on mixed turns rather than whole-turn classification, or (b) the predicate_double_fail rule should not terminate when at least one of the two fails contained slot-fillable content visible to the Meta LLM.

anomalies: Run1 bootstrap response had 'job' pre-covered with confidence=0.1 (covered_slots=[job], open_slots=7), but run2 bootstrap had ALL slots open with confidence=0.0. Non-determinism in bootstrap envelope shape between two back-to-back sessions with identical request.

Run2 T2 took 16.1s vs ~1s for triage-only turns — the latency jump is the signature of the Meta-LLM Q-gen path firing (i.e., triage said 'on-topic, capture job, ask next slot'). Run1 T2 took 1.24s with no slot capture — triage said 'off-topic, re-ask Q1' without invoking Meta. This confirms the two-LLM pipeline shape (Blackbox triage gates Meta Q-gen).

CAS index 'creator' field reveals the architecture: every accepted turn writes 'turn-N-triage' (triage call) + optionally 'turn-N' (Q-gen call). Termination signature on disk = 'turn-N-triage' appearing twice with NO 'turn-N' in between — visible in both run1 (T2 double-triage) and run2 (T3 double-triage).

Mixed-content triage failure: same answer template ('substantive_answer + 反问_meta_question') was triaged differently in run1 vs run2 — run1 T2 failed, run2 T2 passed. Indicates triage classifier is noisy at the on/off-topic boundary.
